step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("most common site of esophageal carcinoma global incidence")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

First state if you agree/disagree with prior conclusion, add one new reason, then give your final answer choice in box format.
I agree with the prior conclusion that the most common site of esophageal carcinoma is the lower two-thirds of the esophagus. New evidence confirms that adenocarcinoma, now the predominant histologic type in Western countries, arises primarily in the distal esophagus and gastroesophageal junction—a region within the lower two-thirds. This aligns with known risk factors such as chronic GERD and Barrett’s esophagus, which are strongly associated with this location.
\boxed{\text{C}}
